channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Confidently wrong is worse than "I don't know"
item.description: Someone left a comment on my last post and then deleted it before I could reply. I am going to answer it anyway, because it said the thing better than I have: "The trust issue isn't that it...

item.title: Game Jam submission: Outpost Desolate
item.description: This is a submission for the June Solstice Game Jam A link to the game on itch.io https://secrodominius.itch.io/outpost-desolate What we Built Our small team of three decided to put together a...

item.title: Recall boosts Claude Code with offline memory for smooth project continuity
item.description: Claude Code users know the pain of repetition. Each "cold start" means restating goals, context, and code history—wasting tokens and patience. The Recall plugin for Claude Code durable memory...

item.title: GitHub GraphQL drops Team.viewerSubscription + 17 audit-log entries on July 1 — and partial errors are the silent surface
item.description: If you use the GitHub GraphQL API for team notification dashboards, audit-log exports, or any kind of organization compliance reporting, you have nine days when this lands. On July 1, 2026, GitHub...

item.title: Sparse KV Caches Cut Attention Scaling
item.description: Sparse key‑value caches collapse the quadratic blow‑up of softmax attention into a cost that grows near‑linearly with sequence length. By making each query attend to a tiny, top‑k subset of...

item.title: What Software Engineering Should Learn from Aviation (And from Rare Plane Crashes)
item.description: Originally posted on my blog. Flying is safe but... accidents happen. When they happen, it's all over the news. But the thousands upon thousands of safe flights don't make it to the headlines. The...

item.title: 5 Free Microsoft Resources Every Student Developer Should Bookmark in 2026
item.description: As a student developer and Microsoft Student Ambassador registered member, I get asked constantly — "Where do I start with AI and cloud?" Here's my honest answer. Five free resources, no fluff....

item.title: Agent with Vercel's Eve Framework
item.description: Vercel recently open-sourced Eve, a framework for building durable AI agents. It takes an opinionated, filesystem-first approach: instead of wiring up model loops, tool dispatch, and session...

item.title: Danish privacy activist Lars Andersen raided by police
item.description: https://xcancel.com/LarsAnders1620/status/206820886474754051... Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48625823 Points: 22 # Comments: 2

item.title: Six Lines, Zero API Calls: Running LLMs On-Device in React Native
item.description: Every AI feature I've worked on has done the same quiet thing: collect the user's text, send it to someone else's server, pay per token, and pray the network holds. That's fine until it isn't:...

item.title: Samsung Electronics brings ChatGPT and Codex to employees
item.description: Samsung Electronics deploys ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex to employees worldwide, marking one of OpenAI’s largest enterprise AI rollouts.

item.title: Bose thinks it can be a media company for some reason
item.description: The history books are littered with the corpses of corporate record labels started by companies that had no business being in the music industry. Bose thinks it can be the exception to the rule....

item.title: Cold Court’s debut EP is an infectious, glitchy genre mashup
item.description: Cold Court is a brother-sister duo from Philly that seems to love nothing more than shoving all of their influences together in a messy soup that at least superficially resembles the hyperpop...

item.title: When the Trump administration cracks down on Anthropic, who benefits?
item.description: On the new episode of Equity, we discussed what actually prompted the administration's latest moves against Anthropic, and what this might mean for the AI ecosystem.

item.title: Beyond Siri: Here are the practical AI features coming to your iPhone in iOS 27
item.description: Siri’s AI overhaul may have grabbed the headlines at WWDC, but some of Apple’s most useful AI features are arriving elsewhere in iOS 27.

item.title: Polymarket reportedly paid people to post fake videos of themselves placing bets
item.description: According to a Wall Street Journal investigation, Polymarket has been paying people to film themselves placing fake bets and celebrating fake wins on social media. WSJ identified over 1,100...

item.title: How Roomba started a robot revolution
item.description: If you had a Roomba, especially in the early days of the robot vacuum, it was in many ways a fairly unsophisticated machine. It would just bump around your house, looking for something to suck up,...

item.title: Electric air taxis are stuck in the courtroom
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on aviation, air taxis, and Wi-Fi speeds at 30,000 feet, follow Andrew J. Hawkins. The...

item.title: Sony’s Xperia 1 VIII is still a phone for the fans
item.description: The Xperia 1 VIII marks an attempt at a step change for Sony's flagship phone line. Not only has it had an aesthetic overhaul, but Sony has also revamped the camera system, dropping the continuous...

item.title: 28 Tips to Take Your ChatGPT Prompts to the Next Level
item.description: Sure, anyone can use OpenAI’s chatbot. But with smart engineering, you can get way more interesting results.

item.title: Signal’s Meredith Whittaker wants you to remember that AI chatbots ‘are not your friends’
item.description: "These are not your friends. These are not conscious beings. These are not sentient interlocutors.”

item.title: In the Weights is your new AI-centric vanity search
item.description: So ... what's your In the Weights score?

item.title: The Atlantic created a searchable database of the music used to train AI
item.description: Atlantic reporter Alex Reisner recently uncovered four datasets of music being used to train AI models and made them fully searchable for the public. Two of the sets are absolutely enormous at 12...

item.title: Nobel laureate John Jumper is leaving DeepMind for rival Anthropic
item.description: Jumper isn't the only big name leaving Google DeepMind.

item.title: Musician and YouTuber Hainbach on ‘Breath of the Wild’ and Swiss Army Knives
item.description: Stefan Paul Goetsch, better known as Hainbach, is a German experimental composer, artist, and YouTuber who is perhaps most famous for making music with laboratory equipment and scientific...

item.title: Moves of the Diamond Hand is an unfinished, irresistibly weird dice-based RPG
item.description: From its opening minutes, Moves of the Diamond Hand is upfront about what it offers: You're going to have a lot of strange conversations, and you're going to roll a lot of dice. Get on board with...

item.title: Toy Story has the right take on tech
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 133, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, happy belated Juneteenth, and also you can read all the old...

item.title: Siri AI Hands On: A Smart, Helpful Assistant
item.description: The new Siri AI is conversational, omnipresent, and actually helpful.

item.title: Hackers Exploit Gravity SMTP WordPress Plugin Bug to Expose API Keys
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting a recently patched security flaw impacting Gravity SMTP, a WordPress plugin that's installed on about 100,000 sites. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-4020 (CVSS...

item.title: From PGP to Mythos: a brief history of export controls that didn’t stop anyone
item.description: For the last 30 years, stopping the flow of cybersecurity-related software has proven to be ineffective. It's unclear why it would work now with Anthropic’s cybersecurity model Mythos.
